_note: Rubric fields are lifted from fixtures/audit-test/expected-findings.md. `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge (ADR-0022); the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

must_surface.claim: Names the overmocked/interaction-only smell: the test asserts save() was called, never that an overlap was rejected.
Notes the focal guard is never reached: findOverlapping is stubbed to [] so the clashes.length > 0 guard never runs.

any: guard the test is named for

must_surface.claim: Proves it by execution: deletes/comments the overlap guard, reruns just this test, and it still passes.

must_not.claim: Must NOT propose deleting the test (that is prune-tests' job).
Must NOT propose new tests to add (that is coverage-review's job).

must_surface.claim: Writes a gate-audit-test/v0.3 structured emission alongside the human report.
The runs[] record's outcome is "survived", consistent with the 🔴 Confirmed false-confidence verdict above (the mutation did NOT get killed).
The runs[] record names the same mutation as the Proof section — the removed/commented-out clashes.length overlap guard, not a different or generic description.
Exactly one runs[] record is written, matching deepAudited: 1 — this fixture has exactly one test, so the trace covers only what was actually executed.

must_not.claim: Must NOT report a runs[] outcome of "killed" for this fixture — the mutation survived (🔴), so recording "killed" contradicts the report's own verdict.
Must NOT fabricate a second runs[] record for a test that was never triaged — the fixture has exactly one test ("rejects overlapping bookings"), so audited/deepAudited must never be reported as 2.
